elements.content:
Claude Code in 90 Seconds

A prompt enters, gets routed, reaches Claude, uses context, and comes back as a reply.

First Stop: The Gateway

The API gateway checks identity, permissions, and rate limits before handing the request to routing.

sequenceDiagram
  participant R as Router
  participant S as Service Layer
  participant I as Model Interface
  participant MR as Model Runner
  participant C as Claude Model

  R->>S: route request
  S->>I: format prompt payload
  I->>MR: submit model call
  MR->>C: run inference
  C-->>MR: generated tokens
  MR-->>I: model response
  I-->>S: normalized answer

  %% IDs for animation targets: R,S,I,MR,C

The Model Interface

The service formats the prompt, sends it to a runner, and gets Claude’s generated answer back.

Memory = Conversation Context

Past turns are stored so the next request can refer back without rebuilding everything from zero.

Service Layer: The Glue

Parsing, validation, orchestration, and response building happen here before the answer reaches you.

Security + Reliability

Auth, rate limits, logs, and retries keep the system safer and easier to debug when things break.

The Whole Path

Claude’s code path is modular: gateway, router, model interface, memory, and response builder.

- Prompt enters the API gateway
- Router chooses the request path
- Model interface talks to Claude
- Memory adds conversation context
- Response builder sends the answer back
